assumptions: The issue is limited to hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk arising from an intragroup dividend receivable.
The dividend receivable is being considered as a potential hedged item or as part of a hedged exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting as an available accounting model for recognised assets and liabilities, and an intragroup monetary item with foreign currency risk can qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when that risk is not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 includes forecast transactions as eligible hedged items, and specifically allows the foreign currency risk of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction to qualify in consolidated financial statements when it will affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model, with the effective portion recognised in other comprehensive income.
